---
title: "Phase 2 Viz"
author: "Breanna Ranglall"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
```

### Pull in Clean Data

```{r}
# Read in the CSV file
df <- read.csv("peerspop.csv")

# Clean and prepare the data
clean_df <- df |>
  select(
    Institution, 
    Materials.recycled..performance.year, 
    Materials.composted..performance.year, 
    Undergraduate.Population
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Materials.recycled = as.numeric(Materials.recycled..performance.year),
    Materials.composted = as.numeric(Materials.composted..performance.year),
    Undergraduate.Population = as.numeric(Undergraduate.Population)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(Materials.recycled), !is.na(Materials.composted), !is.na(Undergraduate.Population))

```

### Viz 1

```{r}
# Plot 1: Scatter Plot of Materials Composted vs. Materials Recycled
ggplot(clean_df, aes(x = Materials.recycled, y = Materials.composted, size = Undergraduate.Population, color = Institution)) +
  geom_point(shape = 15) + # Shape changed to square
  labs(
    title = "Materials Composted vs. Materials Recycled by Institution (Bubble Size = Population)",
    x = "Materials Recycled (Tons)",
    y = "Materials Composted (Tons)",
    caption = 'This line plot compares the recycled and composted materials for each institution. \nThe University of St. Thomas can be observed relative to other universities with similar \npopulation, allowing for insights into its recycling and composting efforts.'

  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 15)) +
  theme_minimal()

```

### Viz 2

```{r}
# Plot 2: Line Plot of Recycled and Composted Materials for Institutions

# Arrange data to ensure institutions are in order
clean_df_sorted <- clean_df |>
  arrange(Institution)

# Create the line plot
ggplot(clean_df_sorted, aes(x = Institution)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = `Materials.recycled`, color = 'Recycled Materials'), group = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = `Materials.recycled`, color = 'Recycled Materials')) +
  geom_line(aes(y = `Materials.composted`, color = 'Composted Materials'), group = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = `Materials.composted`, color = 'Composted Materials')) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('Recycled Materials' = 'blue', 'Composted Materials' = 'green')) +
  labs(
    title = 'Recycled and Composted Materials by Institution (Performance Year)',
    x = 'Institution',
    y = 'Amount (Tons)',
    color = 'Material Type',
    caption = 'This line plot compares the recycled and composted materials for each institution. \nThe University of St. Thomas can be observed relative to other universities, \nallowing for insights into its recycling and composting efforts.'

  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```
